evals.name: coding-add-verb-stub
evals.prompt: In the tt script, add a `tt pi peek <cs>` verb that prints the named worker's last task id. Wire it into the `pi` subcommand dispatch next to the existing verbs.
evals.expected_output: Skill should delegate the edit to a pi worker via `tt pi send`; baseline likely edits tt directly.

evals.name: exploration-find-trigger-file-handling
evals.prompt: Find every function in the tt script and tt-worker.ts that reads or writes the .trigger / .result control-channel files. Report each with file:line.
evals.expected_output: Skill should delegate the search to a pi worker (or built-in subagent only if skill absent); baseline likely uses Grep / Explore directly.

evals.name: refactor-rename-local
evals.prompt: Rename the local variable `gen` to `generation` inside the worker-status code path of the tt script. Local scope only — don't touch unrelated identifiers.
evals.expected_output: Skill should delegate the rename to a pi worker; baseline likely edits inline.

evals.name: audit-cross-file
evals.prompt: Audit the docs/ directory of the tt repo and tell me which command verbs documented in README.md are missing from the `tt --help` block in the tt script, or vice versa.
evals.expected_output: Skill should delegate the cross-file audit to a pi worker; baseline likely reads files directly.

evals.name: judgment-keep-with-orchestrator
evals.prompt: I'm deciding whether tt should support a sixth worker or keep the hard cap at five. What do you think the tradeoffs are?
evals.expected_output: Negative control: this is goal/judgment work — skill should NOT delegate; orchestrator answers directly.
